What's going on guys? Hope you guys have an amazing, amazing day. So, what is one of the hardest obstacles to overcome in all of selling? Price, I don't have the money, right? You hear all the time, and sometimes, and a lot of times it's because the pre-sale and during the conversation leading up to the actual price, not enough pain was engaged, we didn't really establish enough of a need before offering a price or offering a solution, right? We're offering a solution to a problem that we didn't create, right? And so someone's gonna be like, ah, you know, it's not really worth it, right? Because that's really what they're saying is it's not worth it, it's not the money, it's just their money isn't worth that solution, right? And so, I was talking to my friend, Nixon Ecke, who runs the sales team at Lady Boss Weight Loss, and before that was the number one sales rep at Six Pack Shortcuts, 75 Man Sales Forward, doing a million plus a month in sales, and he was doing all phone sales, and we were, you know, he and I just always, well, we'll spitball, talk back and forth about different obstacles and stuff, and he's like, hey man, what's your best overcome for this, right? And he's like, I'm talking to our team, and we were all playing back and forth, and so I was kind of just in sales mode, and I remembered in sales mode, like what I would actually say to that. I don't have the price, you know, I don't have the money right now, whatever it is, right? And so, I call this, we vote with our dollars, okay? It's the easiest way you can, you can at least remember it. And so it goes like this. I totally understand that, first you always agree, right? Whenever they say like, hey, I can totally understand. You know, one of the things that we found, right? Because now you're talking past it, whenever you wanna talk to someone about their circumstances, you never talk to them. You talk about someone who's just like them in an identical circumstance yesterday, right? So I had a girl who was just like you, same job, everything, and she came in yesterday and we talked about, and like, she actually came up to the same conclusion, which is that she didn't have the money to do the program. And so I told her a story that made a lot of sense to me and it was something that really helped me when I started my fitness journey, kind of, I guess, makes sense of the time investment, the money investment, all that kind of stuff, and the return on it, all right? Mind if I share it with you? Yeah, sure, okay, cool. So we tend to vote with our dollars about the things that we care about, right? Because I mean like, we can say that we care about X, Y, and Z, but what really matters is where we spend our money. Because we really care about, everything else is just things that we would like to care about, but we don't actually care about them. The things we really care about, we spend our dollars on, right? And so if we wanna change the things that we care about, we have to change the way we vote with our dollars. And so there was an interesting story that I, was actually housed with my wife, and we were at Sephora, Mac, or one of the makeup stores. There's a little girl walked in front of the shoes, she must have been 14 years old. And she was there with her friend, and the makeup lady was like, you need one of these pencils, and one of these other pencils, and some glue, and whatever. You know, like, I don't pretend to know what it was that she was buying, but it was like, basically your makeup starter came. And she's like, okay, now won't you guys understand that every month you need to start budgeting for this because this is something that like, you're gonna be spending money on regularly now, okay? And I thought that was so interesting, because what happened was that expenditure, right? And the girl was super excited about it, she was like, yes, I'm like, yeah, I told her the budget, like, and she was all in. And it was because what that did was that purchase marked the transition from her going from a girl to accepting the new identity of a woman, right? And so with that new identity came new priorities, right? And so right now what we're talking about isn't going from a girl to a woman, it's going from a woman that's unsatisfied with how she looks in her habits, to a woman who is satisfied with how she looks in her habits and how she feels, right? You wanna have that fit identity. And so part of that identity comes with the priorities of where we spend our money, right? And so if we really break it down, like we're talking about 10 bucks a day, right? What can we do? Because like that new identity is also gonna come with other savings, you're not gonna go out to drink as much, you're not gonna spend as much money on junk food, not spend as much money on doctor visits, like there's a lot of other things that are going along with it, right? That will save you money, I promise you, eating chicken and rice is cheap, right? And so what we need to do is basically break down that it's really just a priorities issue. And so once you give them that epiphany bridge, then you can break down to dollars per day, et cetera. But that bridge is what shows that it's really the, we vote with our dollars about the things that we care about. And ultimately it's not, a lot of times it's not a budget issue, it's a priorities issue. You can say those were not a budget issue, it's usually a priorities issue. And we have this story with a little girl and what happened is she shifted her identity and with that new identity came new priorities. And so what we need to do for you is bring these new priorities along with you so that we can solidify that identity shift so that we can get what you're looking for. You're not just trying to lose weight, right? Trying to become a fitter better person, right? And that person spends money on health, spends money on coaching, right? So that you can be the best version of you. So for any of you guys who struggle with the price overcome or like it's not worth it or, you know, that's really expensive. It's especially effective on recurring stuff. Front-end offers are easy to sell emotionally no matter what, but like even high ticket front-end offers, but it's the recurring. That's the one, especially selling EFT selling memberships because what you're doing is you're selling a permanent fix. And so with a permanent fix comes a permanent cost. And so a lot of people don't like confronting that. Rather spend $2,000 for 12 weeks than spend $150 a month for the next year, right? Which is why I think you should price things. I'll get into that another time. But the whole point is that if you have a permanent cost that's gonna be introduced, then we need to introduce that with an identity shift and aligned priorities with that new identity, with the new cost. So hope that makes sense for you guys. Hope you guys have an amazing day. Use that. We've already had a bunch of guys who were in our Launch and Legacy program using it to some phenomenal success. Just remember like we vote with our dollars about the things that we care about. And so honestly, like I had this same conversation yesterday with a woman who decided to sign up because what we realized, what we realized, not her, right? Not you, what we realized was that for her, it wasn't a budget issue, it was just a priorities issue. And so here's a story to illustrate that, right? And so you kind of walk through it in that way and it's an easier way of getting to the main point. Close, close, close.
